summary: The main claim is: I believe that including the embodiment/interface of an agent within our measure/concept of its intelligence inflates the concept and leads to confusion.
The sensors that an agent/system possesses, its actuators, processing speed, data storage capacity, and so on, are things that I believe should be understood separately from intelligence.

If the main claim is true, then linguistic AI is, in some sense, prior to robust value alignment.

The main claim is: To model realistic social dynamics, MIRL methods must account for suboptimal human reasoning and behavior.
This research identifies and compares mechanisms in MIRL methods which a) handle noise, biases and heuristics in agent decision making and b) model realistic equilibrium solution concepts.
